preamble: Part of Grimm's Law: PIE voiced plosives became voiceless plosives.

examples.note: Final-obstruent devoicing: /d/ is realized as [t] word-finally.
Turkish final consonant devoicing: underlying /d/ → [t] word-finally. Cf. 'gidiyor' (going) showing /d/ in non-final position.
PIE *d → PGmc *t via Grimm's Law. Cf. Latin decem preserving original *d.

sources: Campbell, L. (2013). Historical Linguistics: An Introduction. MIT Press.
